The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-27 22:45PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong is a city of duelling narratives, but deep down we know the truth
  • Hong Kong’s greenhouse gas emissions fall for third year, but concern groups say more work needed
  • Hong Kong police arrest 6 men in connection with predawn shooting in Central; 3 others detained in separate firearm cases

  • HK Express flight makes emergency landing at Hong Kong airport after report of smoke in cockpit, all 47 passengers and 7 crew members unharmed

  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teens allegedly involved in clash that led to cleaner’s death cannot be held liable for murder, judge rules

  • US-led rare earths pact satisfies South Korea’s ‘definite need’ to cut China dependency

  • Cryptocurrency is ‘biggest Ponzi scheme in human history’, Chinese execs from state-backed blockchain drive say

  • China’s May industrial profits kept tumbling, raising calls for more government support

  • China changes law so it can act if country is insulted at sporting events

  • Asean special envoy appeals to Myanmar junta to spare Suu Kyi prison
  • Singapore’s return to tighter Covid rules cannot be ruled out, deputy PM Wong says
  • South Korean Navy Seal broke the law to fight in Ukraine, calls it ‘the Wild West’
  • Japan’s snub of talks with South Korea on sidelines of Nato summit a ‘missed opportunity’
  • Is the US setting up an ‘Asia-style Nato’? North Korea thinks so
  • Japan’s Kishida makes veiled China reference as he warns G7 of ‘attempts to change the status quo by force’

  • Australia hosts ‘historic’ Nasa launch – first from commercial site outside US
  • China’s education crackdown spurs Japan moves for international schools
  • Indonesia ‘democracy, human rights at risk’ as row over criminal code heats up
  • 3 killed as Amtrak train hits truck, derails in Missouri
  • US man shoots Subway worker dead over ‘too much mayo on sandwich’
  • At least 13 killed in Russian missile strike on crowded shopping centre, Ukraine says
  • Britain ‘sick man of Europe’ is hurtling towards breaking point after being battered by Brexit and Covid-19
  • Nato to boost rapid reaction force in ‘biggest overhaul since Cold War’
  • Russia to spend US$14.5 billion on aircraft amid sanctions
  • Ukraine: ‘Help us end war by winter’, Zelensky urges world leaders – as Russia pours fire on stronghold
  • Nato to pledge aid to Baltics and Ukraine, urge Türkiye to let in Sweden and Finland
  • Roe vs Wade: America’s rival camps dig in for fight after abortion ruling
  • Ukraine war: Russia slips into default zone as payment deadline expires
  • UN drugs report: Cannabis use has risen with legalisation and Covid-19 lockdowns
  • Hong Kong, Singapore investors filling void in London property left by mainland Chinese retreat
  • Beijing and Wuhan join local Chinese governments to bolster EV sales by offering subsidies to buyers
  • Qianhai offers cheap housing and full-time jobs in bid to attract more Hongkongers to work in special economic zone
  • Tencent takes a US$7.4 billion market knock as Prosus prepares to cut stake to fund stock buy-back, sells JD.com as non-strategic asset
  • Britain’s pensions agency to remove Chinese-made security cameras, ban new purchases
  • China needs to act fast to close the widening supply-demand gap for low-carbon talent, experts say
  • Hong Kong can be a hub, not just for capital going into China, but also funds coming out of mainland for M&A, ex-SFC chairman says
  • How China can protect its economy – and the world’s – amid US recession headwinds
  • Alibaba, Haidilao lead Hong Kong stocks to near three-month high on China recovery signals as Beijing eases lockdown
  • Hongkongers join bespoke Japan property tours to scout for investment opportunities amid yen’s slump
  • Taiwan stocks facing ‘big bully’ prompt Goldman retreat as cross-strait tensions inflict 20 to 40 per cent fund losses
  • How parents can help their children get ready for a digital future
  • London’s Regent Street struggles to draw shoppers back after pandemic, amid rise in online retail
  • Minions: The Rise of Gru movie review – Steve Carell, Michelle Yeoh and Jean-Claude Van Damme enliven enjoyable second prequel to Despicable Me
  • The 10 best K-dramas of 2022 … so far, from Our Blues and Link: Eat, Love, Kill to Money Heist: Korea – Joint Economic Area
  • Who are Lionesses? Queer K-pop boy band who want to be the LGBTQ+ icons they wish they’d had growing up
  • Pandemic anger, ‘panger’, is a real mental health concern. Expert suggests 4 steps to deal with your emotions
  • Thailand’s temples the star of luxury boat trip from Bangkok to the ancient kingdom of Ayutthaya
  • Actress Fala Chen on Irma Vep, Shang-Chi, parallels with Maggie Cheung and the Louis Vuitton catsuit she had to wear on set
  • School in China ditches sleeping on desks at nap time for health reasons and lets students set up their own tents to sleep in
  • Chicken, the ‘fowl of five virtues’ in Chinese lore, and why Singapore got into a flap over a shortage of the birds
  • How an old empty Hong Kong building became a homeless shelter and could serve as the template to get more people off the streets
  • Meet Muhammad Ali’s grandson, Biaggio Ali Walsh: unlike the boxing legend, the American footballer debuted with his first MMA match this month, and once modelled for Versace with Irina Shayk
  • 10 richest golf stars of 2022 – net worths, ranked: Tiger Woods is the sport’s first billionaire, but how do Rory McIlroy, Jordan Spieth and Phil Mickelson tee up?
  • Inside Kim Kardashian’s US$150 million private jet, aka Kim Air: the cashmere-clad Gulfstream plane seats 18 and is pricier than even Jeff Bezos and Elon Musk’s rides
  • Meet Cy Busson, heartthrob son of supermodel Elle Macpherson and billionaire Arpad Busson: he’s already appeared on Vogue’s cover, has PDA snaps on Instagram and loves travelling
  • Virgil Abloh’s legacy at Louis Vuitton’s Paris Fashion Week menswear show: the bold spring/summer 2023 collection is the fashion house’s first not created by the late designer
  • 8 best modern Chinese restaurants in Hong Kong: from favourites Man Ho and Mott 32, to celebrity chef Vicky Cheng’s Wing and fine dining Cantonese at Rùn
  • 5 K-pop reunions that prove 2nd generation groups are forever: Kara surprised fans with comeback rumours, 2PM released a mini-album, and 2NE1’s Coachella gig
  • 6 facts about Seo In-guk, star of Netflix’s Café Minamdang – the ‘chameleon’ K-drama actor loves food, music, his mum … and older women
  • 6 best cities for executive digital nomads: Miami, Lisbon, Dubai and Barcelona top Savills’ first ranking of locations ideal for long-term remote working
  • Inside South Indian star Nayanthara’s US$22 million net worth – the Netrikann actress lavishes husband Vignesh Shivan with gifts, boasts a huge real estate empire and gets around by private jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
